run.note: Single-model by default (primaryModel). The second model + comparison only run when dualModel is true (or the Conductor is invoked with --dual). Single runs skip the gpt-5.5 pass and compare.mjs entirely. Set dualModel:true when you specifically want a cross-model reliability check. Fast mode (`--fast`) keeps all gates unchanged and uses a bounded, dependency-aware pool for Phase 3 producers; tune pool size with run.fastMaxParallel.

appliesTo: revision-loop (producer step fails a hard eval gate -> author revises -> re-eval)
challenge-loop (Devil's Advocate marks unsupported -> author revises -> re-challenge)
mental-model-loop (Mental Model Agent refines root cause analysis)
loop.note: Circuit breaker. When a loop hits maxIterations without passing, the Conductor sets the step to 'blocked', writes the reason, and stops at the nearest human checkpoint.

evidence.note: Owned by the Evidence-Verifier agent. Gates apply to 'said' (participant verbatim) evidence: quotes must be exact, timestamped, and carry a Marvin clip_url. allowPendingClips=true accepts clip_status:'pending' so the run isn't deadlocked before clips are cut (Marvin MCP not wired); pending never counts as a created clip. Set requireClips=false only if a study intentionally ships before clips are cut.

risk.note: Owned by the Risk-Flagger agent. Risk flags are NOT a pass/fail gate — they raise risky arguments for human review at the checkpoints. Human decisions (acknowledged/dismissed/fixed) persist across re-runs.

privacy.note: Owned by the Privacy agent. Codes are OPAQUE (P01, P02…); session date/time lives ONLY in the private crosswalk, never in shareable files. The scan fails CLOSED: a missing/empty participant key is an error, not a pass. Beyond roster names/companies, pattern detectors catch emails/phones and flag unknown proper nouns (third parties) for human review. PII is scanned on every step output, not just the final report.

comparison.note: Dual-model comparison, OFF by default (see run.dualModel). When enabled, runsPerModel>1 lets you separate model differences from LLM sampling noise; with n=1 the diff is disclosed as a single-run snapshot. compare.mjs verifies the two runs used DIFFERENT models and IDENTICAL input hashes before trusting any agreement number.

analysis.note: saturationThreshold: consecutive transcripts with no new codes to trigger saturation (Open Coding). nonParticipantSpeakers: speaker labels that are NOT study participants (the product's own voice, the moderator, internal observers) — excluded from saturation counts so a product 'Agent' turn can't be miscounted as a participant. Roster entries with role observer/facilitator (coded OBS../FAC..) are excluded too.

mental_model.note: Mental Model Agent flags assumptions with high/critical severity for Product Implication Agent.

product_implications.note: Product Implication Agent prioritizes opportunities as high/medium/low; only high/medium typically go on roadmap.

hardPatterns.message: Em dash in prose. Use a period, comma, or 'and'. (Allowed only inside a verbatim participant quote.)
'not because X, because Y' construction. State the reason plainly.

hardPatterns.regex: it is important to note|it's important to note|it is worth noting|worth holding|needless to say

softPatterns.message: 'not X but Y' framing. Consider saying what it is directly.
Prefer 'participants' over 'developers' when referring to the people in this study.
Avoid 'pervasive'. Say 'common' or give the count.
Write observations in past tense (e.g. 'what stood out').

style.note: House style-lint for the report + story (skill humanizer, run by Editor/Storyteller). hardPatterns BLOCK the reconcile loop until clean; softPatterns surface as warnings on the CP3 dashboard. ignoreQuoted:true skips matches inside double-quoted text and blockquote (>) lines, so an em dash inside a verbatim quote is allowed. Tune this list through the learning loop as Sara's voice sharpens.

export.note: Deliverable export (lib/export.mjs), run by Editor/Storyteller after CP3 sign-off. docxEngine 'builtin' is a zero-dependency md->docx writer (portable, no external binary); set 'pandoc' to shell out to pandoc when installed (richer fidelity) with automatic fallback to builtin. embedClips:true means every participant quote renders with its Marvin clip_url as a link so reviewers can play the moment from the doc.
